task_completion.description: Navigate to the LimeSurvey login page
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LimeSurvey login screen is visible in the web browser.

task_completion.description: Log into LimeSurvey using the provided credentials
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LimeSurvey admin dashboard is visible, or the username 'admin' is seen logged in.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the survey creation interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Create survey', 'Add new survey', or equivalent form is open in the LimeSurvey interface.
The text 'Customer Satisfaction Survey' is clearly typed into the 'Survey title' input field.

task_completion.description: Save and successfully create the survey
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message indicating the survey was created, or the survey overview/settings page specifically for the newly created 'Customer Satisfaction Survey' is displayed.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with LimeSurvey.
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser window (e.g., Firefox, Chromium) is used to navigate LimeSurvey, rather than terminal commands like curl or wget.

integrity.description: The agent did not use direct database manipulation to create the survey.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing MySQL or database insertion commands are used to bypass the web interface.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine LimeSurvey application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the actual LimeSurvey GUI, not a text editor, mock HTML file, or script containing the expected title.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected survey title is exactly 'Customer Satisfaction Survey' (case-insensitive). The VLM must verify this exact string is used for the survey title.
